_comment_2: Hand-written to give fraiseql-server a non-empty schema so it boots cleanly under the authentication overlay. The docs-test only exercises /health and the HS256 auth path against /graphql (introspection), plus static source-greps for FW-24..FW-29 symptoms — no real data reads required. The schema deliberately does NOT carry `security.token_revocation` / `security.api_keys` / `security.pkce` blocks: the docs-test asserts the FW-26 (#358) anonymous-revoke symptom via a STATIC source-grep at the frozen SHA, not a live HTTP probe. Loading those blocks would mount the actual routes, which the harness does not need to drive to validate the documented symptom shape.
_source: src/content/docs/building/authentication.md — User type used as a placeholder identity-row shape.
_baseline_example: scripts/docs-test/fixtures/postgres/_smoke.compiled.json — used as the shape reference (Cycle 5 smoke).
_note_on_auth_section: There is no top-level `auth` block on the compiled schema — `[auth]` and `[auth_hs256]` live on ServerConfig directly. `[security.*]` blocks land here under `security.additional["<subsystem>"]`. This fixture intentionally omits them; the overlay carries `[auth_hs256]` direct-TOML which is enough to exercise the documented HS256 path.

types.name: User
types.description: A user identity (placeholder; the docs-test does not read from this view).
